name: webacoo
title: webacoo

commands.name: webacoo

arguments.raw: -f FUNCTION	PHP System function to use

arguments.description: Single command execution mode (-t and -u are required)
arguments.raw: -e CMD	Single command execution mode (-t and -u are required)

arguments.description: HTTP method to be used (default is GET)
arguments.raw: -m METHOD	HTTP method to be used (default is GET)
-d DELIM	Delimiter (default: New random for each request)
-p PROXY	Use proxy (tor, ip:port or user:pass:ip:port)

commands.raw_help:
root@kali:~# webacoo -h
 
 	WeBaCoo 0.2.3 - Web Backdoor Cookie Script-Kit
 	Copyright (C) 2011-2012 Anestis Bechtsoudis
 	{ @anestisb | anestis@bechtsoudis.com | http(s)://bechtsoudis.com }
 
 
 Usage: webacoo [options]
 
 Options:
 -g		Generate backdoor code (-o is required)
 
 -f FUNCTION	PHP System function to use
 	FUNCTION
 		1: system 	(default)
 		2: shell_exec
 		3: exec
 		4: passthru
 		5: popen
 
 -o OUTPUT	Generated backdoor output filename
 
 -r 		Return un-obfuscated backdoor code
 
 -t		Establish remote "terminal" connection (-u is required)
 
 -u URL	Backdoor URL
 
 -e CMD	Single command execution mode (-t and -u are required)
 
 -m METHOD	HTTP method to be used (default is GET)
 
 -c C_NAME	Cookie name (default: "M-cookie")
 
 -d DELIM	Delimiter (default: New random for each request)
 
 -a AGENT	HTTP header user-agent (default exist)
 
 -p PROXY	Use proxy (tor, ip:port or user:pass:ip:port)
 
 -v LEVEL	Verbose level
 	LEVEL
 		0: no additional info (default)
 		1: print HTTP headers
 		2: print HTTP headers + data
 
 -l LOG	Log activity to file
 
 -h		Display help and exit
 
 update	Check for updates and apply if any

apt_metadata.description:
Web backdoor cookie script kit
Scripts for creating Web backdoors using cookies, with module support

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use webacoo because ### webacoo.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `webacoo [options]` as documented upstream for webacoo.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair webacoo with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `webacoo --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [web] in the Kali Linux category. Fits when the engagement needs: ### webacoo.
usage_sections: Install with: sudo apt install webacoo
